14: // Top module
15: module adbg_lintonly_top
16: #(
17:     parameter ADDR_WIDTH = 32,
18:     parameter DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter AUX_WIDTH = 6
20: )
21: (
22:         // JTAG signals
23:         input  logic           tck_i,
24:         input  logic           tdi_i,
25:         output logic           tdo_o,
26:         input  logic           trstn_i,
27: 
28:         // TAP states
29:         input  logic                   shift_dr_i,
30:         input  logic                   pause_dr_i,
31:         input  logic                   update_dr_i,
32:         input  logic                   capture_dr_i,
33: 
34:         // Instructions
35:         input  logic                       debug_select_i,
36: 
37:         input  logic                        clk_i,
38:         input  logic                        rstn_i,
39: 
40:     output logic                        lint_req_o,
41:     output logic   [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0]     lint_add_o,
42:     output logic                        lint_wen_o,
43:     output logic   [DATA_WIDTH-1:0]     lint_wdata_o,
44:     output logic [DATA_WIDTH/8-1:0]     lint_be_o,
45:     output logic    [AUX_WIDTH-1:0]     lint_aux_o,
46:     input  logic                        lint_gnt_i,
47:     input  logic                        lint_r_aux_i,
48:     input  logic                        lint_r_valid_i,
49:     input  logic   [DATA_WIDTH-1:0]     lint_r_rdata_i,
50:     input  logic                        lint_r_opc_i
51:     );
52: 
53: 
54:    wire                tdo_axi;
55:    wire                tdo_cpu;
56: 
57:    // Registers
58:    reg [`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-1:0] input_shift_reg;  // 1 bit sel/cmd, 4 bit opcode, 32 bit address, 16 bit length = 53 bits
59:    reg                          [4:0] module_id_reg;    // Module selection register
60: 
61: 
62:    // Control signals
63:    wire               select_cmd;      // True when the command (registered at Update_DR) is for top level/module selection
64:    wire         [4:0] module_id_in;    // The part of the input_shift_register to be used as the module select data
65:    reg          [1:0] module_selects;  // Select signals for the individual modules, number of modules = 2(AXI and CPU)
66:    wire               select_inhibit;  // OR of inhibit signals from sub-modules, prevents latching of a new module ID
67:    wire         [1:0] module_inhibit;  // signals to allow submodules to prevent top level from latching new module ID
68: 
69:     integer j;
70: 
71:    ///////////////////////////////////////
72:    // Combinatorial assignments
73: 
74:     assign select_cmd   = input_shift_reg[`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-1];
75:     assign module_id_in = input_shift_reg[`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-2:`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-6];
76: 
77: //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
78: // Module select register and select signals
79: //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
80: 
81:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
82:     begin
83:         if (~trstn_i)
84:             module_id_reg <= 5'h0;
85:         else if(debug_select_i && select_cmd && update_dr_i && !select_inhibit)       // Chain select
86:             module_id_reg <= module_id_in;
87:     end
88: 
89:     always_comb
90:     begin
91:   		if ( module_id_reg == 0 )
92:             module_selects = 2'b01;
93:         else
94:             module_selects = 2'b10;
95:     end
96: //////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
97: 
98: 
99: ///////////////////////////////////////////////
100: // Data input shift register
101: ///////////////////////////////////////////////
102: 
103: always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
104: begin
105:   if (~trstn_i)
106:     input_shift_reg <= 'h0;
107:   else if(debug_select_i && shift_dr_i)
108:     input_shift_reg <= {tdi_i, input_shift_reg[`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-1:1]};
109: end
110: ///////////////////////////////////////////////
111: 
112: 
113: //////////////////////////////////////////////
114: // Debug module instantiations
115: 
116: // Connecting LINT module
117:     adbg_lint_module #(
118:        .ADDR_WIDTH(ADDR_WIDTH),

147:         .lint_r_aux_i  ( lint_r_aux_i   ),
148:         .lint_r_valid_i( lint_r_valid_i ),
149:         .lint_r_rdata_i( lint_r_rdata_i ),
150:         .lint_r_opc_i  ( lint_r_opc_i   )
151:     );
152: 
153:     assign select_inhibit = | module_inhibit;
154: 
155:     /////////////////////////////////////////////////
156:     // TDO output MUX
157: 
158:     always @ (module_id_reg or tdo_axi or tdo_cpu)
159:     begin
160:         if (module_id_reg == 0)
161:             tdo_o <= tdo_axi;
162:         else if (module_id_reg == 1)
163:             tdo_o <= tdo_cpu;
164:         else
165:             tdo_o <= 1'b0;
166:     end
167: 
168: 
169: endmodule

27:     input  logic         clk_i,
28:     input  logic         rst_ni,
29:     XBAR_TCDM_BUS.Master jtag_lint_master
30: );
31: 
32: 
33:     // Top module
34:     adbg_lintonly_top #(
35:         .ADDR_WIDTH ( ADDRESS_WIDTH ),
36:         .DATA_WIDTH ( DATA_WIDTH    )
37:     ) dbg_module_i (
38:         // JTAG signals
39:         .tck_i          ( tck_i                    ),
40:         .tdi_i          ( tdi_i                    ),

738:         .tck_i        ( jtag_tck_i        ),
739:         .tdi_i        ( jtag_axireg_tdi_i ),
740:         .trstn_i      ( jtag_trst_ni      ),
741:         .tdo_o        ( jtag_axireg_tdo_o ),
742:         .shift_dr_i   ( jtag_shift_dr_i   ),
743:         .pause_dr_i   ( 1'b0              ),
744:         .update_dr_i  ( jtag_update_dr_i  ),
745:         .capture_dr_i ( jtag_capture_dr_i ),
746:         .lint_select_i( jtag_axireg_sel_i ),

65: `include "adbg_defines.v"
66: `include "adbg_or1k_defines.v"
67: 
68: // Module interface
69: module adbg_or1k_module #(
70: 		parameter NB_CORES = 4
71:     ) (
72:     // JTAG signals
73:     input  logic                       tck_i,
74:     output logic                       module_tdo_o,
75:     input  logic                       tdi_i,  // This is only used by the CRC module - data_register_i[MSB] is delayed a cycle
76: 
77:     // TAP states
78:     input  logic                       capture_dr_i,
79:     input  logic                       shift_dr_i,
80:     input  logic                       update_dr_i,
81: 
82:     input  logic                [56:0] data_register_i,
83:     input  logic                       module_select_i,
84:     output logic                       top_inhibit_o,
85:     input  logic                       trstn_i,
86: 
87:     // Interface to OR1200 debug unit
88:     input                              cpu_clk_i,    // 'bus' style interface to SPRs
89:     input                              cpu_rstn_i,
90:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [15:0] cpu_addr_o,
91:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [31:0] cpu_data_i,
92:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [31:0] cpu_data_o,
93:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_bp_i,
94:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_stall_o,
95:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_stb_o,
96:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_we_o,
97:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_ack_i
98: );
99: 
100: 
101: 
102:    // Registers to hold state etc.
103:    reg [31:0]      address_counter;     // Holds address for next Wishbone access
104:    reg [5:0]      bit_count;            // How many bits have been shifted in/out
105:    reg [15:0]      word_count;          // bytes remaining in current burst command
106:    reg [3:0]      operation;            // holds the current command (rd/wr, word size)
107:    reg [31:0]      data_out_shift_reg;  // parallel-load output shift register
108:    reg  [2:0] internal_register_select;  // Holds index of currently selected register
109:    wire [NB_CORES-1:0]                   internal_reg_status;  // Holds CPU stall and reset status - signal is output of separate module
110: 
111: 
112:    // Control signals for the various counters / registers / state machines
113:    reg                       addr_sel;          // Selects data for address_counter. 0 = data_register_i, 1 = incremented address count
114:    reg                       addr_ct_en;        // Enable signal for address counter register
115:    reg                       op_reg_en;         // Enable signal for 'operation' register
116:    reg                       bit_ct_en;         // enable bit counter
117:    reg                       bit_ct_rst;        // reset (zero) bit count register
118:    reg                       word_ct_sel;       // Selects data for byte counter.  0 = data_register_i, 1 = decremented byte count
119:    reg                       word_ct_en;        // Enable byte counter register
120:    reg                       out_reg_ld_en;     // Enable parallel load of data_out_shift_reg
121:    reg                       out_reg_shift_en;  // Enable shift of data_out_shift_reg
122:    reg                       out_reg_data_sel;  // 0 = BIU data, 1 = internal register data
123:    reg [1:0]                   tdo_output_sel;  // Selects signal to send to TDO.  0 = ready bit, 1 = output register, 2 = CRC match, 3 = CRC shift reg.
124:    reg                       biu_strobe;      // Indicates that the bus unit should latch data and start a transaction
125:    reg                       crc_clr;         // resets CRC module
126:    reg                       crc_en;          // does 1-bit iteration in CRC module
127:    reg                       crc_in_sel;      // selects incoming write data (=0) or outgoing read data (=1)as input to CRC module
128:    reg                       crc_shift_en;    // CRC reg is also it's own output shift register; this enables a shift
129:    reg                       regsel_ld_en;    // Reg. select register load enable
130:    reg                       intreg_ld_en;    // load enable for internal registers
131:    reg                       cpusel_ld_en;
132: 
133: 
134:    // Status signals
135:    wire                   word_count_zero;   // true when byte counter is zero
136:    wire                   bit_count_max;     // true when bit counter is equal to current word size
137:    wire                   module_cmd;        // inverse of MSB of data_register_i. 1 means current cmd not for top level (but is for us)
138:    wire                   biu_ready;         // indicates that the BIU has finished the last command
139:    wire                   burst_instruction; // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid burst instruction for this module
140:    wire                   intreg_instruction; // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid internal register instruction
141:    wire                   intreg_write;       // True when the input_data_i reg has an internal register write op
142:    wire                   rd_op;              // True when operation in the opcode reg is a read, false when a write

248:    // These have generic read/write/select code, but
249:    // individual registers may have special behavior, defined here.
250: 
251:    // This is the status register, which holds the reset and stall states.
252: 
253:    assign status_reg_wr = (intreg_ld_en & (reg_select_data == `DBG_OR1K_INTREG_STATUS));
254: 
255:    adbg_or1k_status_reg #(
256: 			.NB_CORES(NB_CORES)
257: 			) or1k_statusreg_i (
258:                      .data_i(status_reg_data),
259:                      .we_i(status_reg_wr),
260:                      .tck_i(tck_i),
261:                      .bp_i(cpu_bp_i),
262:                      .trstn_i(trstn_i),
263:                      .cpu_clk_i(cpu_clk_i),
264:                      .cpu_rstn_i(cpu_rstn_i),

371: 		`STATE_pause_ir:         pause_ir = 1'b1;
372: 		`STATE_exit2_ir:         exit2_ir = 1'b1;
373: 		`STATE_update_ir:        update_ir = 1'b1;
374: 		default: ;
375: 	endcase
376: end

378: /**********************************************************************************
379: *                                                                                 *
380: *   End: TAP State Machine                                                        *
381: *                                                                                 *
382: **********************************************************************************/
383: 
384: 
385: 
386: /**********************************************************************************
387: *                                                                                 *
388: *   jtag_ir:  JTAG Instruction Register                                           *
389: *                                                                                 *
390: **********************************************************************************/
391: reg [`IR_LENGTH-1:0]  jtag_ir;          // Instruction register
392: reg [`IR_LENGTH-1:0]  latched_jtag_ir; //, latched_jtag_ir_neg;
393: wire                  instruction_tdo;
394: 
395: always @ (posedge tck_pad_i or negedge trstn_pad_i)
396: begin
397:   if(trstn_pad_i == 0)
398:     jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:0] <= `IR_LENGTH'b0;
399:   else if (test_logic_reset == 1)
400: 	jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:0] <= `IR_LENGTH'b0;
401:   else if(capture_ir)
402:     jtag_ir <= 4'b0101;          // This value is fixed for easier fault detection
403:   else if(shift_ir)
404:     jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:0] <= {tdi_pad_i, jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:1]};
405: end
406: 
407: assign instruction_tdo = jtag_ir[0];  // This is latched on a negative TCK edge after the output MUX
408: 
409: // Updating jtag_ir (Instruction Register)
410: // jtag_ir should be latched on FALLING EDGE of TCK when capture_ir == 1
411: always @ (posedge s_clk_neg or negedge trstn_pad_i)
412: begin
413:   if(trstn_pad_i == 0)
414:     latched_jtag_ir <= `IDCODE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
415:   else if (test_logic_reset)
416:     latched_jtag_ir <= `IDCODE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
417:   else if(update_ir)
418:     latched_jtag_ir <= jtag_ir;
419: end
420: 
421: /**********************************************************************************
422: *                                                                                 *
423: *   End: jtag_ir                                                                  *
424: *                                                                                 *
425: **********************************************************************************/
426: 
427: 
428: 
429: /**********************************************************************************
430: *                                                                                 *
431: *   idcode logic                                                                  *
432: *                                                                                 *
433: **********************************************************************************/
434: reg [31:0] idcode_reg;
435: wire        idcode_tdo;
436: 
437: always @ (posedge tck_pad_i or negedge trstn_pad_i)
438: begin
439:   if(trstn_pad_i == 0)
440:     idcode_reg <= `IDCODE_VALUE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
441:   else if (test_logic_reset)
442:     idcode_reg <= `IDCODE_VALUE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
443:   else if(idcode_select & capture_dr)
444:     idcode_reg <=  `IDCODE_VALUE;
445:   else if(idcode_select & shift_dr)
446:     idcode_reg <=  {tdi_pad_i, idcode_reg[31:1]};
447: 
448: end
449: 
450: assign idcode_tdo = idcode_reg[0];   // This is latched on a negative TCK edge after the output MUX
451: 
452: /**********************************************************************************
453: *                                                                                 *
454: *   End: idcode logic                                                             *
455: *                                                                                 *
456: **********************************************************************************/
457: 
458: 
459: /**********************************************************************************
460: *                                                                                 *
461: *   Bypass logic                                                                  *
462: *                                                                                 *
463: **********************************************************************************/
464: wire  bypassed_tdo;
465: reg   bypass_reg;  // This is a 1-bit register
466: 
467: always @ (posedge tck_pad_i or negedge trstn_pad_i)
468: begin
469:   if (trstn_pad_i == 0)
470:      bypass_reg <=  1'b0;
471:   else if (test_logic_reset == 1)
472:      bypass_reg <=  1'b0;
473:   else if (bypass_select & capture_dr)
474:     bypass_reg<= 1'b0;
475:   else if(bypass_select & shift_dr)

506:     `MBIST:             mbist_select            = 1'b1;    // Mbist test
507:     `DEBUG:             debug_select            = 1'b1;    // Debug
508:     `BYPASS:            bypass_select           = 1'b1;    // BYPASS
509:     default:            bypass_select           = 1'b1;    // BYPASS
510:   endcase
511: end
512:

514: /**********************************************************************************
515: *                                                                                 *
516: *   Multiplexing TDO data                                                         *

519: reg tdo_mux_out;  // really just a wire

522:           debug_tdo_i or bs_chain_tdo_i or mbist_tdo_i or bypassed_tdo or

14: module safe_domain
15:     #(
16:         parameter FLL_DATA_WIDTH = 32,
17:         parameter FLL_ADDR_WIDTH = 32
18:     )
19:     (
20:         input  logic             ref_clk_i            ,
21:         output logic             slow_clk_o           ,
22:         input  logic             rst_ni               ,
23:         output logic             rst_no               ,
24: 
25:         output logic             test_clk_o           ,
26:         output logic             test_mode_o          ,
27:         output logic             mode_select_o        ,
28:         output logic             dft_cg_enable_o      ,
29: 
30:         output logic             sel_fll_clk_o,
31: 
32:         input  logic             jtag_tck_i           ,
33:         input  logic             jtag_trst_ni         ,
34:         input  logic             jtag_tms_i           ,
35:         input  logic             jtag_tdi_i           ,
36:         output logic             jtag_tdo_o           ,
37: 
38:         output logic             soc_tck_o            ,
39:         output logic             soc_trstn_o          ,
40:         output logic             soc_tms_o            ,
41:         output logic             soc_tdi_o            ,
42: 
43:         output logic             jtag_shift_dr_o      ,
44:         output logic             jtag_update_dr_o     ,
45:         output logic             jtag_capture_dr_o    ,
46: 
47:         output logic             axireg_sel_o         ,
48:         output logic             axireg_tdi_o         ,
49:         input  logic             axireg_tdo_i         ,
50: 
51:         input  logic       [7:0] soc_jtag_reg_i       ,
52:         output logic       [7:0] soc_jtag_reg_o       ,
53: 
54:         //**********************************************************
55:         //*** PERIPHERALS SIGNALS **********************************
56:         //**********************************************************
57: 
58:         // PAD CONTROL REGISTER
59:         input  logic [127:0]     pad_mux_i            ,
60:         input  logic [383:0]     pad_cfg_i            ,
61: 
62:         output logic [47:0][5:0] pad_cfg_o            ,
63: 
64:         // GPIOS
65:         input  logic [31:0]      gpio_out_i           ,
66:         output logic [31:0]      gpio_in_o            ,
67:         input  logic [31:0]      gpio_dir_i           ,
68:         input  logic [191:0]     gpio_cfg_i           ,
69: 
70:         // UART
71:         input  logic             uart_tx_i            ,
72:         output logic             uart_rx_o            ,
